channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Tren de Aragua believed to be behind murder of immigration official near border
item.description: Police in the Mexican state of Chihuahua believe that the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua may be behind the murder of a Mexican immigration official just south of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump is heading to Capitol Hill as GOP struggles to agree on legislative strategy ahead
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is heading to Capitol Hill late Wednesday to meet privately with Republican senators as House and Senate GOP leaders are straining to come up with a strategy for tackling his legislative priorities as the party takes power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Dems to join Republicans to advance anti-illegal immigration bill named after Laken Riley
item.description: At least 9 Senate Democrats are expected to vote to advance the Laken Riley Act, which requires federal authorities to detain illegal immigrants found guilty of theft-related crimes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FLASHBACK: Trump has long history of warning Newsom over 'terrible' wildfire prevention
item.description: President-elect Trump repeatedly slammed California Gov. Gavin Newsom during his fist term in the Oval Office for Newsom's handling of wildfires and prevention.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump blames Newsom for California's raging wildfires
item.description: The wildfires that started on Tuesday have killed two people and destroyed more than 1,000 structures.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Paxton secures court order to halt border wall material auctions ahead of Trump’s inauguration
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton secured a court order preventing the Biden administration from selling or disposing of border wall materials, following allegations of auctions violating a court mandate.
item.dc:creator: Holly Galvan Posey, Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: Ohio Supreme Court hears arguments in watchdog group's records dispute with state attorney general
item.description: The Ohio Supreme Court has heard arguments in a long-running public records dispute involving state Attorney General Dave Yost and a watchdog group.
item.dc:creator: Julie Carr Smyth, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: MAGAfest Destiny? Trump flexes his muscles with repeated talk of American expansionism
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's turning up the volume on his calls to acquire Greenland, regain control of the Panama Canal and make Canada the nation's 51st state are forcing world leaders to react and respond, and likely will foreshadow the blunt effect his second administration will have
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FEMA to pay for more firefighters as California blazes burn beyond capacity
item.description: The funding will provide federal reimbursements for up to 75% of “eligible firefighting costs" incurred as California seeks to shore up its firefighting force and put out the wildfire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate approves committee assignments, tees up confirmation hearings next week
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday approved its organizing resolution for the 119th Congress, officially setting the composition of committees and greenlighting nomination hearings to take place in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Freedom Caucus holds its fire on Johnson’s strategy for passing Trump agenda
item.description: Members of the conservative House Freedom Caucus are holding their fire despite Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) bucking their preferred strategy for passing President-elect Trump’s agenda, arguing that the ultimate product is more important than the configuration.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department says it plans to release only part of special counsel's Trump report for now
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department said Wednesday that it intends to release special counsel Jack Smith’s findings on Donald Trump’s efforts to undo the results of the 2020 presidential election but will keep under wraps for now the rest of the report focused on the president-elect's hoarding
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dems accused of 'stonewalling' Tulsi Gabbard confirmation after GOP demands quick hearing
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard and the Trump transition team have accused Senate Intelligence Committee Vice Chairman Mark Warner of "stonewalling" her confirmation process.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Germany's leader says all countries must respect existing borders, in a rebuttal of Trump
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German Chancellor Olaf Scholz said Wednesday that the principle of inviolability of borders applies to every country, however powerful, and suggested that expansionist comments by U.S. President-elect Donald Trump are meeting with “incomprehension” among European leaders.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says he is still considering pre-emptive pardons for Trump targets Liz Cheney, Fauci and others
item.description: President Biden said he was still considering pre-emptive pardons for President-elect Donald Trump's political foes, including former GOP Congresswoman Liz Cheney and Dr. Anthony Fauci, during his final interview with a print publication before leaving the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US will announce $500M in weapons to be sent to Ukraine
item.description: RAMSTEIN AIR BASE, Germany (AP) — The U.S. is set to provide Ukraine an additional $500 million in weapons quickly pulled from its existing stockpiles as the Biden administration works to get Kyiv in a stronger negotiating position before President-elect Donald Trump takes office, two U.S.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: IRS has improved taxpayer services but is slow to resolve ID theft, an independent watchdog says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The IRS boosted taxpayer services through Democrats’ Inflation Reduction Act but still faces processing claims from a coronavirus pandemic-era tax credit program and is slow to resolve certain identity theft cases, according to an independent watchdog report released Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Drug dealers could be charged with murder under new Virginia fentanyl plan
item.description: Virginia Republicans, facing a one-seat minority, hope to tackle the fentanyl crisis with stringent punishments including murder charges for some drug dealers, lawmakers say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Carter and Biden's long friendship had wrinkles. It will be on display a final time with a eulogy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Joe Biden is the consummate Washington insider. Jimmy Carter was anything but. Yet the 46th and 39th U.S.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump files emergency petition to Supreme Court to prevent sentencing in NY v. Trump
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Wednesday morning filed an emergency petition to the United States Supreme Court in an effort to block his sentencing in New York v. Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump asks the Supreme Court to block sentencing in his hush money case in New York
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is asking the Supreme Court to call off Friday’s sentencing in his hush money case in New York.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sen. Warren presses Hegseth to answer questions about his past actions and statements
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Sen. Elizabeth Warren is pressing Pete Hegseth, President-elect Donald Trump's pick for defense secretary, to answer additional questions about his past actions and statements before next Tuesday's confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's Trudeau announces resignation after nearly a decade as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday after nearly a decade in power, bowing to rising discontent over his leadership and growing turmoil within his government signaled by the abrupt departure of his finance minister.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: The drama-free proceeding stood in stark contrast to four years ago when pro-Trump rioters violently stormed the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Congress certifies Trump's election win despite weather threat
item.description: Congress certified President-elect Donald Trump’s election win on Monday as Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) plowed ahead with the vote despite a snowstorm set to hit Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, PIX 11

item.title: Child tax credit expansion proposed: What it means for New Yorkers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – A proposal to expand New York's child tax credit would give families more money per child, Gov. Kathy Hochul announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Harris readies to certify Trump’s election win – and her decisive defeat
item.description: Kamala Harris is readying on Monday to certify the 2024 election results, affirming President-elect Trump’s victory as well as her decisive defeat to him after a whirlwind run for commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Lombardo's State of the State speech set for Jan. 15
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo will give his 2025 State of the State Address on Wednesday, Jan. 15, at 6 p.m., according to a Friday statement from the governor's office.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump will be sentenced Jan. 10 in New York case, days before his inauguration
item.description: In a decision Friday, New York Judge Juan Merchan noted that his inclination was to not impose a sentence of incarceration.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News
